A group of friends want to share some money and they have forty dollars to divide between ten friends, but then two friends leave so now they have to divide it between eight is not correct, they still have to divide forty dollars but now between the remaining eight no, the correct is forty dollars between the original ten but two leave, so between eight, no. The correct is that the forty dollars was going to be divided by ten but now is divided between eight no. The correct is the original forty dollars between ten friends now two friends left so there are eight friends but that is forty dollars between ten friends so ten is the divisor and the dividend is forty so forty divided by ten is four dollars per friend but two friends left so the forty dollars is still divided between ten so forty divided by ten is four dollars per friend, how much money will each of the original ten friends get

A group of friends want to share some candy equally, if they have six bags of candy, and each bag contains three pounds of candy, and they already gave away five pounds of candy, how many pounds of candy can they share

A farmer is harvesting wheat from his field, he has four sections of the field, and each section yields five baskets of wheat, if he has already collected seven baskets of wheat, how many baskets of wheat does the farmer have in total

A teacher is preparing lesson plans for her class, she needs to make five copies of a worksheet, and each copy requires three sheets of paper, if she already has eight sheets of paper, how many sheets of paper does the teacher have in total

A cyclist is training for a race, she rides six laps around the track, and each lap is four kilometers long, if she has already ridden nine kilometers, how many kilometers has the cyclist ridden in total

A chef is making a recipe that requires five cups of flour, and each cup of flour needs to be mixed with two cups of sugar, if the chef already has eleven cups of sugar, how many cups of sugar does the chef have in total

situational:chain:3.text: If a bookstore has forty eight books on a shelf and they remove twelve books to make room for new arrivals, how many books are left on the shelf after they add twenty more books

A chef needs to package three hundred and sixty cookies into boxes that hold eighteen cookies each, and then she needs to add seven more boxes to the shipment, how many cookies will the chef package in total

A group of friends want to share some money they found, if they have one hundred and twenty dollars and they give thirty two dollars to a charity, how much money will they have left after they divide the remaining money equally among nine friends

A teacher has one hundred and eighty stickers to give to her students, and she gives fifteen stickers to each of her eight students, and then she buys thirty more stickers, how many stickers will the teacher have left in total

A person is planning a road trip that is two hundred and seventy miles long, and they want to drive at a speed of thirty five miles per hour, and they will take a thirty minute break, how many hours will the trip take in total if they drive for four hours before taking the break
A company has ninety six employees and they hire eight new employees every month for three months, how many employees will the company have in total after the three months

A person has two hundred and fifty dollars in their account and they withdraw fifty dollars to buy a gift, and then they deposit twenty five dollars, how much money will they have left in their account after they spend thirty two dollars on a dinner

A farmer has one hundred and twenty five kilograms of wheat and he sells forty five kilograms to a customer, and then he buys thirty kilograms more wheat, how many kilograms of wheat will the farmer have left in total

Tom has been saving money for a new bike and has thirty five dollars in his piggy bank. He wants to buy a bike that costs eighty seven dollars. If he mows lawns for eight dollars per lawn, how many lawns does he need to mow to have enough money for the bike?
A bookshelf has five shelves and each shelf can hold nine boxes of books. If the bookshelf is currently empty, how many boxes of books can be placed on it in total?

A bakery is making a special batch of cookies for a holiday sale. They need to package forty eight cookies into boxes that hold six cookies each. How many boxes can they fill with the cookies they have?
A group of friends want to share some candy equally. If they have forty two pieces of candy and there are seven friends, how many pieces of candy will each friend get?
A water tank can hold sixty five gallons of water. If thirty seven gallons of water are already in the tank, how many more gallons can be added to fill the tank?
A person is planning a road trip that is two hundred sixty five miles long. If they want to drive at a rate of forty five miles per hour, how many hours will the trip take?
A store is having a sale on shirts. A shirt that originally costs fifty dollars is on sale for twenty percent off. How much will the shirt cost during the sale?

situational:chain:4.text: If a bakery sells forty eight cupcakes at seven dollars each and they need to pay twelve dollars in delivery fees, how much money will the bakery have after paying for delivery

A group of friends want to split a bill of seventy three dollars, if they give the waiter a twenty five percent tip and there are seven people in the group, how much will each person pay

A water tank can hold sixty five thousand gallons of water, if thirty two thousand gallons are already in the tank and eight thousand gallons are added every day for four days, how many gallons of water will be in the tank after four days

If a car rental company charges forty five dollars per day to rent a car and a customer rents a car for nine days, but the company gives a ten percent discount for long term rentals, how much will the customer pay in total

A bookshelf has five shelves, and each shelf can hold twenty five books, if the bookshelf is currently empty and a librarian wants to put eighty seven books on it, how many books will be left over after filling the bookshelf
A cyclist is training for a race and wants to ride sixty eight miles per day for five days, but she also wants to ride an extra twelve miles on the last day, how many miles will she ride in total

A restaurant is having a sale on burgers, and they are offering a discount of fifteen percent on all orders over fifty dollars, if a customer orders sixty two dollars worth of burgers, how much will they pay after the discount

Tom has been saving money for a new bike and has twenty five dollars in his piggy bank. He wants to buy a bike that costs sixty dollars. His grandmother gives him thirty five dollars for his birthday. How much more money does Tom need to buy the bike after his grandmother's gift
A bookshelf has five shelves and each shelf can hold seven boxes. If the bookshelf is currently empty, how many boxes can be placed on it in total

A bakery sells forty eight cupcakes in the morning and seventy two cupcakes in the afternoon. They have to package the cupcakes in boxes that hold twelve cupcakes each. How many boxes are needed to package all the cupcakes sold
A group of friends want to share some candy equally. If they have sixty eight pieces of candy and there are four friends, how many pieces of candy will each friend get
A water tank can hold fifty gallons of water. If twenty gallons of water are already in the tank and fifteen more gallons are added, how many more gallons can be added to the tank
A student has ninety five dollars to spend on school supplies. She buys a backpack for twenty seven dollars and a set of pens for eight dollars. How much money does the student have left

A car travels twenty two miles in the city and thirty eight miles on the highway. If the car uses twelve miles per gallon of gas in the city and fifteen miles per gallon of gas on the highway, and gas costs three dollars per gallon, how much did the car owner spend on gas for the city part of the trip plus the highway part of the trip if the car uses the respective mileage for each part

situational:chain:5.text: A bookstore is having a sale where everything is twenty five percent off, and a customer buys a book that originally costs sixty dollars, then the customer also buys a bookmark for five dollars, how much will the customer pay in total for the book and the bookmark

A person is planning a road trip and wants to budget for gas, they estimate that they will use twenty percent of their one hundred fifty dollar gas budget for the first day of driving, then they will also spend twelve dollars on snacks, how much will they have left in their gas budget after the first day

A company is having a meeting and they need to order catering for their employees, the catering company charges thirty five dollars per person and they expect twenty five percent of the employees to attend, if there are one hundred employees, then they also need to pay a twenty dollar delivery fee, how much will the company pay in total for the catering and delivery

A person is shopping online and finds a shirt that they want to buy, the shirt originally costs forty five dollars, but it is on sale for twenty percent off, then they also need to pay eight dollars for shipping, how much will the person pay in total for the shirt and shipping

A group of friends want to go to a concert and they need to buy tickets, the tickets originally cost seventy five dollars each, but they are able to get twenty five percent off, then they also need to pay a fifteen dollar service fee, how much will each person pay in total for their ticket and the service fee

A person is planning a party and they need to order food and drinks, they estimate that they will spend thirty percent of their one hundred dollar food budget on appetizers, then they will also spend eighteen dollars on decorations, how much will they have left in their food budget after buying the appetizers and decorations

A company is having a sale where everything is ten percent off, and a customer buys a TV that originally costs one hundred thirty dollars, then the customer also buys a warranty for twenty dollars, how much will the customer pay in total for the TV and the warranty

If a travel agent gives a fifteen percent discount on a hotel room that costs one hundred twenty dollars and the customer has to pay an eight dollar service fee, how much will the customer pay in total

A restaurant is having a special promotion where they are offering twenty percent off all orders over fifty dollars, if a customer orders food worth one hundred ten dollars and has to pay a twelve dollar delivery fee, how much will the customer pay in total

A store is having a sale where they are offering twenty five percent off all shoes, if a pair of shoes originally costs one hundred thirty five dollars and the customer has to pay a seven dollar sales tax, how much will the customer pay in total

If a person wants to buy a ticket to a concert that costs forty eight dollars and the ticket website charges a twenty percent service fee plus a five dollar payment processing fee, how much will the person pay in total

A gym is offering a ten percent discount on their monthly membership which costs sixty dollars, if a customer signs up for a year and has to pay a twenty five dollar registration fee, how much will the customer pay in total for the first year

A person is buying a phone that costs one hundred forty dollars and the store is offering a fifteen percent discount, plus the customer has to pay a ten dollar activation fee, how much will the customer pay in total

If a customer orders food worth one hundred dollars from a restaurant that charges a twenty percent tip, and the customer also has to pay a four dollar delivery fee, how much will the customer pay in total
